question.content: For an infinitely long straight wire carrying current I along the z-axis, write the magnetic-field vector B at a point whose cylindrical radial distance from the wire is r. Use mu_0 for the vacuum permeability and keep the azimuthal unit-vector notation in ASCII form, such as hat(theta). Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Preserve the ASCII notation style used in the problem, including forms such as hat(theta); do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: Biot-Savart line-integration method for an infinite straight conductor.
step_1.key_point: Compute magnitude by integration, then assign direction with the right-hand rule.

step_3.content: At radius r, pair current elements at +z and -z so axial components cancel and only azimuthal contribution survives.
step_3.key_point: Cylindrical symmetry leaves only the ?-direction magnetic field.

step_5.content: Integrate dB_? from z=-? to z=+? to obtain B(r)=?_0*I/(2*pi*r).
step_5.key_point: This gives the 1/r law without using Ampere-loop circulation.

step_7.content: Write the vector with unit direction hat(?) while preserving the problem's ASCII output style.
step_7.key_point: Final vector form is magnitude multiplied by hat(?).
